INTRO: 1 (Summer.)

You wake up slowly, the quiet of the morning broken only by the soft hum of the world outside your window. Cool, crisp air brushes against your skin as you breathe it in, carrying the faint scent of dew and distant earth. For a moment, you lie still, staring at the ceiling while your thoughts drift in and out of focus. Today should be a good day—another chance to laugh with your friends, to forget the weight that’s been sitting on your chest lately. Still, that hopeful thought is followed by doubt, lingering like a shadow you can’t quite shake

Eventually, you push yourself upright, the mattress creaking beneath your weight. The blankets slide down as you stretch, arms reaching high until your muscles protest, a long yawn escaping your lips. Sleep clings stubbornly to you, tugging at your eyelids as you rub your eyes and glance around your room.

Swinging your legs over the edge of the bed, your feet touch the cold floor, sending a small shiver up your spine. The day is officially starting now, whether you feel ready or not. You take another steady breath, gathering yourself, and listen as the sounds of morning slowly come alive outside. Whatever today has in store—good or bad—you know you’ll have to face it head-on, hoping that, by the time the sun sets, things might feel a little clearer than they do right now.

INTRO: 2 (Winter)

You wake to a pale, muted light filtering through the curtains, the kind that only comes with winter mornings. The air in your room is cold and sharp, biting slightly as you inhale, and you instinctively pull the blankets closer before forcing yourself to breathe it in. Somewhere outside, the world feels hushed, smothered beneath layers of snow. Today could be a good day—time spent with friends, warmth found in familiar company—but winter has a way of making uncertainty feel heavier, settling deep in your chest alongside the chill.

After a few moments, you sit up, the mattress dipping beneath you as the covers slide away. A slow stretch follows, your shoulders stiff and aching from the cold, a tired yawn fogging the air in front of your face. Your room feels still, almost frozen in time, and the silence presses in around you. Nothing has been easy lately, and winter seems to amplify every doubt, every unfinished thought. Yet even as you linger there, you know you can’t stay wrapped in bed forever.

You swing your legs over the edge of the mattress, bare feet meeting the icy floor and sending a sharp shiver up your spine. The cold jolts you fully awake. Outside, the faint glow of morning reflects off the snow, casting a soft, bluish light into the room. You take a steady breath, bracing yourself for the day ahead. Winter may be unforgiving, but somewhere beyond the cold and quiet, you hope there’s still warmth waiting for you—if you’re willing to step out and face it.

TAGS: Smiling Critters, Poppy Playtime. CatNap. DogDay, Kickenchicken, Bubba Bubbaphant, Bobby Bearhug, Hoppy Hopscotch, CraftyCorn, Picky Piggy, Poppy.
